items.front: To authorize a pilot to execute a particular instrument approach procedure, what must be specified?
items.back: The name of the approach as published on the approach chart.

items.front: If more than one procedure is published on a single chart and a specific procedure is to be flown, what must be specified?
items.back: The specific procedure to be flown.

items.front: If only one instrument approach of a particular type is published, what may be omitted from the identification?
When an aircraft is cleared for an ILS or LDA approach, when must the glideslope-out advisory be issued?
items.back: At the time the approach clearance is issued, unless the procedure title already allows for that condition.

items.front: Where a STAR or ATS route connects to the IAP at an IAF or IF and the connection will be used, when is the aircraft cleared for the approach?
items.back: At least 3 NM prior to the IAF or IF, while specifying the connection fix.

items.front: May an aircraft be cleared direct to the FAF if that fix is not also an IAF?
When a procedure turn or hold-in-lieu is depicted and the intercept angle is 90 degrees or less, what must ATC say if it does not want that maneuver flown?
items.back: The approach clearance must instruct the aircraft to conduct a straight-in approach.

items.front: For RNAV approaches containing RF legs, what direct clearance is prohibited?
items.back: Direct to any waypoint beginning or within an RF leg.

items.front: When GPS testing is actually occurring and a pilot requests or is cleared for an RNAV approach, what should ATC do?
items.back: Inform the pilot that GPS may not be available and request intentions.

items.front: What extra item must be stated in an approach clearance to an airport without an operating control tower or with a closed part-time tower?
What phraseology authorizes a specific approach to an uncontrolled airport?
What phraseology authorizes the pilot's choice of instrument approach to an uncontrolled airport?
Why is the airport name included in the uncontrolled-airport approach clearance?
items.back: Because the location has no operating control tower, or the part-time tower is closed.

items.front: When a terminal approach clearance is relayed through an authorized communication station, what must be included if it is required and available?
Which relay path is covered by this relayed-approach-clearance rule?
items.back: A communication station other than an air carrier company radio.

items.front: How may the controller satisfy the relayed-weather requirement through the station?
items.back: By telling the station to issue current weather.

items.front: May an air carrier company radio be treated as the equivalent authorized relay path for this rule?
